plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Reduce QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for trimming.
Buy XLK at 10% to add high-quality tech exposure and diversify.
Increase cash to 18% for capital preservation and buffer.
This plan significantly reduces leverage and concentration.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets capital preservation with a potential ~1.5%/month outlook. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by cutting leverage and reducing concentration. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, but also more modest returns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL to remove a high-risk leveraged ETF, and trim SPAL to 5%.
Reduce QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for trimming.
Buy XLK at 10% to add diversified quality tech exposure.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility.
This plan balances growth potential with risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets risk-first growth with a potential ~2.5%/month outlook. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns, with a focus on sustainable growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL to exit a losing position and remove downtrend exposure.
Reduce QQQI to 10% as it was flagged for trimming.
Add to SMH, increasing to 20%, to press strength in semiconductors.
Buy XLK at 15% and MRVL at 10% to add strong, high R/R tech setups.
Keep a minimal 5% cash buffer to maximize market exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets stretch growth with a potential ~4%/month outlook. It leans into high-scoring tech setups and uses leverage, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of higher volatility, potential for significant losses, and the daily decay of leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce broad index exposure and meet mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Increase QQQI to 30% for enhanced monthly income distributions.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to cut exposure to high-beta single names.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer for stability and low drawdown.
This plan prioritizes steady income and capital preservation.
income.read: This income plan targets parking cash for yield with a potential ~1%/month outlook. It rotates into income-generating ETFs and reduces high-beta exposure. While prioritizing low drawdown and reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account.
